title: seeded-walk reproducibility surface
manager: Make a random walk of a machine exactly reproducible from a seed, and carry that seed in the serialization — so "it did X on run 4711" becomes a claim anyone can replay bit-for-bit instead of a story. This is the reproducibility floor the whole replay-and-proof stack stands on: determinism tests promote straight into conformance vectors, and a bug report can ship the seed that triggers it rather than a paragraph of hand-waving.
cs: seed captured in the serialized form; deterministic PRNG (splitmix lineage) so a walk is a pure function of (machine, seed, stimuli); the M3 determinism tests promote into the C4 vector corpus (WP-1.4, the standing no-normative-feature-without-a-vector rule).
